metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or fold into AC3's verify step) that the `--print-volumes` contract line appears in both script headers, so the header-documentation deliverable is covered by at least one criterion.
findings.finding: The described deliverable 'contract documented in both script headers (compose-up.sh: consumed by config-check.sh check 5; config-check.sh: requires compose-up.sh --print-volumes)' has no covering acceptance criterion — no AC verifies the header documentation is present or correct.
findings.location: What section, final bullet ('The --print-volumes contract is documented in both script headers'); Acceptance Criteria section (AC1–AC4)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3's 'proving command recorded in verify_commands' step to also grep each script header for the contract string, or add a dedicated AC5: 'compose-up.sh header contains "consumed by config-check.sh check 5" and config-check.sh header contains "requires compose-up.sh --print-volumes" — verified by grep in the verify_commands record.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the full AC3 proving command inline in the plan (stub setup, PATH override, canary-file assertion, and expected output assertion) so the plan is self-contained and AC3 is verifiable without consulting `verify_commands`.
findings.finding: AC3's proving command is deferred to an external `verify_commands` artifact not reproduced in the plan, making the plan non-self-contained for this criterion. An executor reading only the plan cannot construct or run the stub proof without finding that external document.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: `mkdir -p /tmp/stubs && for b in docker dnf systemctl; do printf '#!/bin/sh\ntouch /tmp/canary_%s\n' "$b" > /tmp/stubs/$b && chmod +x /tmp/stubs/$b; done && PATH=/tmp/stubs:$PATH bash infra/scripts/compose-up.sh --print-volumes > /tmp/pvols.txt && grep -q gerrit_reviewbot_tickets /tmp/pvols.txt && ! ls /tmp/canary_* 2>/dev/null && echo PASS` — record this as the canonical proving command in the plan body rather than referencing verify_commands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the --print-volumes contract (its purpose, consumer, and why grep/awk text-parsing was rejected) so the design rationale persists in the repo, not just in the ticket.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel inter-script contract (`--print-volumes`) and a new CI enforcement pattern (drift gate with embedded self-tests), but does not include an update to an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or equivalent persistent design document — only transient script-header comments. Future contributors adding volumes or modifying compose-up.sh need to understand why this pattern exists and what the contract guarantees.
findings.location: 'What (mechanism)' section — '--print-volumes contract is documented in both script headers'; 'Alternative considered' section
findings.suggested_fix: Create `infra/docs/adr/0001-compose-drift-gate.md` (or append to an existing CLAUDE.md/DEVNOTES) with: (1) the problem (incident 2731), (2) the decision (--print-volumes mode as single source of truth), (3) the rejected alternative (text-parsing), and (4) the contract (stdout volume names, exit-code semantics, consumed by config-check.sh check 5).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` as an edit target in the 'What (mechanism)' section, alongside `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh`, so the full edit surface (both files modified, not just one) is unambiguous.
findings.finding: infra/scripts/config-check.sh is a required edit target that the plan never explicitly names as such. The plan describes adding an entirely new check (check 5) plus two embedded self-tests to this file, but the 'What (mechanism)' section characterises config-check.sh only as a consumer of --print-volumes, not as a file being modified. An implementer reading the edit targets would not list it.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What (mechanism)' section, add a bullet: '- `infra/scripts/config-check.sh`: add check 5 (external-volume drift gate, two embedded self-tests, --print-volumes invocation)' at the same level as the compose-up.sh and config-check.sh check 5 paragraphs, so both files are listed as concrete edit targets.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
